oneOf.description: Consume arc: place -> transition. Carries a consume pattern.
Produce arc: transition -> place. Carries a produce template.

annotations.description: Freeform consumer annotations. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

description.description: Optional human-readable description. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

capacityPerColorKey.description: Non-behavioral verification bound: the place holds at most 'max' tokens per distinct value of the token-data key(s). NEVER gates firing; the engine ignores it. Checked by the declarative property pass (spec/properties.md; ADR 0019).

key.description: Token-data field name (or array of field names, a composite key) whose distinct values partition the place's tokens for the bound.

max.description: Maximum tokens per distinct key value.

mode.description: 'consume' (default): requires a matching token and removes it on fire. 'inhibit': a zero-test requiring the ABSENCE of a matching token and consuming nothing. 'read': test-without-consume; requires a matching token (gates enablement like consume) and contributes it to the binding, but removes nothing on fire (ADR 0012).

type.description: Token type to match. Must be one of the source place's 'accepts' (enforced structurally).

weight.description: Number of matching tokens the arc requires (default 1 = classical one-token-per-arc). Applies to 'consume' (that many removed) and 'read' (that many present and bound, none removed). Rejected on inhibit arcs (D7, firing-semantics.md; ADR 0012).

correlate.description: Binding-correlated zero-test (anti-join, ADR 0017). Only on mode 'inhibit' (enforced by the parser). The transition is enabled under a candidate binding only if NO matching token in the source place satisfies this CEL over {token: <candidate token's data>, binding: <place-keyed bound-token data>}. Compiled at parse time; a runtime eval error blocks the candidate binding (fail-closed).

cel.description: Inline CEL over two names: 'token' (the inhibit-place candidate's data) and 'binding' (map of source-place name -> list of bound tokens' data, consume- and read-mode arcs). CEL-only; a named-handler variant would amend the handler contract and is deferred (ADR 0017).

marking.description: Map of place name -> tokens held there. Keys are place names; values are arrays of tokens.

accepts.description: Token types ('colors') this place will hold. Tokens of other types never land here.

annotations.description: Freeform consumer annotations. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

description.description: Optional human-readable description. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

port.description: Declares this place a boundary place (composition interface). A port is a place facet, not a separate kind.

type.description: Token type this port carries. Must appear in the enclosing place's 'accepts' (enforced by the parser).

predicate.description: A pure arc-level filter on a single token. Mutually exclusive: inline CEL XOR named pure predicate handler ref (ADR 0002). Absent => match any token of the declared type.

cel.description: Inline Common Expression Language (google/cel-spec) expression. Pure and side-effect-free; evaluates against the candidate token's data.

handler.description: Named pure predicate handler ref, resolved by the registry. Pure: returns boolean, no side effects, single-token.

cel.description: Optional computed fallback (ADR 0023), mutually exclusive with 'data': inline CEL over the single name 'binding' (map of source-place name -> list of bound tokens' data, consume- and read-mode arcs). Evaluated only for a destination/type pair the handler left uncovered; the result must be a JSON object and becomes the emitted token's data. Compiled at parse time; a runtime eval error or non-object result is a deposit-contract violation (D3). Like 'data', it never gives a handlerless transition structural firing behavior.

data.description: Optional literal payload used when a bound passthrough/routing handler returns no token for this destination (Q3). It does not give a handlerless transition structural firing behavior; the template remains a routing contract, not a token factory.

destination.description: The place the produced token lands in. MUST equal this arc's 'to' place (enforced structurally).

type.description: Token type the handler may deposit here. Must be one of the destination place's 'accepts' (enforced structurally).

timer.description: A declarative temporal enablement condition (ADR 0018). The transition is enabled only when the CEL expression holds for the candidate binding, evaluated against the clock place's token plus the bound tokens named in 'bind'. The deadline lives in token data; the engine holds no timer state; time advances only by token injection (ADR 0013).

bind.description: CEL variable name -> place name: exposes the candidate binding's tokens to the expression. Each place must feed the transition through a consume- or read-mode arc, and 'clock' is reserved (both enforced by the parser). Keys are simple identifiers so the CEL string survives composition merge (only the place values are rewritten).

cel.description: The temporal condition: a CEL expression over 'clock' (the clock token's data) and the 'bind' variables (each the data of the first token bound from its place). Compiled at parse (D6); a runtime eval error degrades to condition-false.

clock.description: The clock place: its first token is the time reference, exposed to the CEL expression as the reserved variable 'clock'. An empty clock place means the transition is not enabled. Must name a declared place (enforced by the parser).

maturity.description: Optional Runtime scheduling CEL expression over the same closed environment as 'cel'. It returns the next future monotonic timestamp for a candidate binding; Runtime requires it for native timers, while synchronous Engine use remains compatible without it.

annotations.description: Freeform consumer annotations. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

description.description: Optional human-readable description. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

guard.description: Guard ref: a named handler, possibly impure, evaluated at the transition level over the full input binding (ADR 0002).

handler.description: Optional behavior binding: when present, a nonempty handler ref resolved by the runtime registry (ADR 0003 amendment). Absence means handlerless; null, empty string, implicit same-name refs, and implicit no-op behavior are invalid.

priority.description: Consumed by the built-in opt-in 'priority' firing policy (ADR 0014): among simultaneously enabled transitions the highest priority fires first; absent = 0; ties fall back to declaration order. Ignored by the default 'first-found' policy (ADR 0005).

description: A colored Petri net: places, transitions, arcs, and an optional initial marking.

annotations.description: Freeform consumer annotations. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

arcs.description: Arc-centric: a flat list of directed place<->transition edges. Direction determines consume vs produce (enforced structurally).

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the net. Documentation-only; ignored by the engine (ADR 0011).

initialMarking.description: Optional initial token distribution. Marking is data, not structure; test harnesses may override it.

places.description: All places in the net. Place names are unique (enforced structurally).

title: Net
